problems.title: Sum of Two Numbers
problems.description: Write a function that takes two numbers and returns their sum.
problems.hint: Use the + operator to add the two numbers together.

problems.title: Check Even or Odd
problems.description: Write a function that checks if a number is even or odd. Return 'Even' if the number is even, or 'Odd' if it's odd.
problems.hint: Use the modulo operator (%) to check if a number is divisible by 2.

problems.title: Find Maximum of Three Numbers
problems.description: Write a function that takes three numbers and returns the largest one.
problems.hint: Compare the numbers using if statements or use the max function.

problems.title: Reverse a String
problems.description: Write a function that takes a string and returns it reversed.
problems.hint: You can loop through the string backwards, or use built-in reverse functions.

problems.title: Count Vowels in a String
problems.description: Write a function that counts the number of vowels (a, e, i, o, u) in a given string.
problems.hint: Loop through the string and check if each character is a vowel. Remember to check both uppercase and lowercase.

problems.title: Calculate Factorial
problems.description: Write a function that calculates the factorial of a number. Factorial of n (n!) is the product of all positive integers less than or equal to n.
problems.hint: Use a loop or recursion. Remember that 0! = 1.

problems.title: Check if String is Palindrome
problems.description: Write a function that checks if a string is a palindrome (reads the same forwards and backwards). Return true if it is, false otherwise.
problems.hint: Compare the string with its reversed version, or use two pointers from both ends.

problems.title: Sum of Array Elements
problems.description: Write a function that calculates the sum of all elements in an array of numbers.
problems.hint: Use a loop to iterate through the array and add each element to a running total.

problems.title: Find Largest Number in Array
problems.description: Write a function that finds and returns the largest number in an array.
problems.hint: Keep track of the maximum value as you loop through the array.

problems.title: Convert Celsius to Fahrenheit
problems.description: Write a function that converts a temperature from Celsius to Fahrenheit. Formula: F = (C × 9/5) + 32
problems.hint: Use the formula: multiply by 9/5, then add 32.

problems.title: Find Duplicate Numbers in Array
problems.description: Write a function that finds and returns all duplicate numbers in an array. Return an array of unique duplicates.
problems.hint: Use a hash map or set to track numbers you've seen. Add to result when you find a duplicate.

problems.title: FizzBuzz
problems.description: Write a function that returns an array of strings from 1 to n. For multiples of 3, add 'Fizz'. For multiples of 5, add 'Buzz'. For multiples of both, add 'FizzBuzz'. Otherwise, add the number as a string.
problems.hint: Check divisibility by 15 first (both 3 and 5), then check 3, then 5, then default to the number.

problems.title: Find Second Largest Number
problems.description: Write a function that finds the second largest number in an array. If there's no second largest (all numbers are the same), return -1.
problems.hint: Keep track of the largest and second largest values as you iterate through the array.

problems.title: Remove Duplicates from Array
problems.description: Write a function that removes all duplicate elements from an array and returns a new array with only unique elements in their original order.
problems.hint: Use a Set to track unique elements, or check if element already exists before adding to result.

problems.title: Count Word Frequency
problems.description: Write a function that counts the frequency of each word in a string. Return an object/dictionary with words as keys and their counts as values. Ignore case and punctuation.
problems.hint: Split the string into words, convert to lowercase, then use a map/dictionary to count occurrences.

problems.title: Binary Search
problems.description: Implement binary search to find the index of a target value in a sorted array. Return -1 if not found.
problems.hint: Use two pointers (left and right). Compare middle element with target and adjust pointers accordingly.

problems.title: Merge Two Sorted Arrays
problems.description: Write a function that merges two sorted arrays into one sorted array.
problems.hint: Use two pointers, one for each array. Compare elements and add the smaller one to the result.

problems.title: Rotate Array
problems.description: Write a function that rotates an array to the right by k positions. For example, [1,2,3,4,5] rotated by 2 becomes [4,5,1,2,3].
problems.hint: You can reverse parts of the array, or use extra space to store elements temporarily.

problems.title: Find Missing Number
problems.description: Given an array containing n distinct numbers from 0 to n, find the one number that is missing from the array.
problems.hint: Use the formula: sum of 0 to n = n*(n+1)/2. Subtract the sum of array elements.

problems.title: Two Sum Problem
problems.description: Given an array of integers and a target sum, return the indices of two numbers that add up to the target. Each input has exactly one solution, and you cannot use the same element twice.
problems.hint: Use a hash map to store values you've seen and their indices. Check if (target - current value) exists in the map.
